name: node-is-arrayish
title: node-is-arrayish
summary: Determines if an object can be used as an array
description:
Determines if an object can be used as an array
A Nodejs module to determine if a given object can be used as an array,
thus the object support indexing.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-is-arrayish because Determines if an object can be used as an array.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-is-arrayish as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-is-arrayish with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-is-arrayish --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
